case.title: WHITE STAG MANUFACTURING CO. v. ELLIS

opinion.h: WHITE STAG MANUFACTURING CO. v. ELLIS, 372 U.S. 251 (1963)
WHITE STAG MANUFACTURING CO. v. ELLIS ET AL., MEMBERS OF THE STATE TAX COMMISSION OF OREGON. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF OREGON. No. 708. Decided February 25, 1963.
opinion.p: Walter H. Evans, Jr. for appellant.
Robert Y. Thornton, Attorney General of Oregon, and Carlisle B. Roberts and Gerald F. Bartz, Assistant Attorneys General, for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
opinion.h: PATTERSON v. CITY OF DALLAS, 372 U.S. 251 (1963)
opinion.p: 372 U.S. 251 PATTERSON ET AL. v. CITY OF DALLAS. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF CIVIL APPEALS OF TEXAS, FIFTH SUPREME JUDICIAL DISTRICT. No. 712. Decided February 25, 1963.
Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
H. P. Kucera and Ted P. MacMaster for appellee.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
